title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. glauca
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. glauca, also known as blue tufted hair grass, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including bronchitis, coughs, and digestive issues, and has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can benefit overall health. Herbal preparations of Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. glauca include infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be used to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
